page: Hetzner Cloud cpx22 is a shared-CPU virtual server powered by the QEMU hypervisor and AMD EPYC Genoa hardware. It features 2 shared vCPUs consisting of 2 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 80 GB of local SSD storage. This instance does not bundle any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a strong single-core profile but limited multi-core scaling due to the shared resource model. The stress-ng single-core CPU test places the server in the top 10% with a score of 3059.18, and the Geekbench single-core score is strong at 1974.0. However, multi-core metrics are weak, with Geekbench multi-core scoring 3532.0 and stress-ng multi-core scoring 6053.08, both landing in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is average for large blocks at 70.65 GB/sec read and 57.5 GB/sec write, but weak for small cached blocks.

In application-specific workloads, the server demonstrates average performance in text processing and single-threaded decompression, alongside moderate capabilities in small-scale LLM inference, processing 498.54 tokens per second for a 135M model. Conversely, database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput rank in the lower tiers. The cpx22 is a cost-efficient option for entry-level workloads, development environments, and lightweight background tasks that benefit from strong single-threaded bursts but do not require sustained multi-core performance or high memory density.
description: Hetzner Cloud cpx22 is an entry-level, shared-CPU virtual server built on the x86_64 AMD EPYC Genoa architecture. It features 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of local SSD storage, operating under the QEMU hypervisor. Benchmarks show a stark contrast between its top-tier single-core performance and its weak multi-core and memory performance, which fall into the bottom 25%. While it handles single-threaded tasks and lightweight text processing adequately, it struggles with intensive database operations and static web serving. This resource profile makes the cpx22 a cost-effective option for development environments, low-traffic applications, and lightweight background utilities.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx22 is a shared-CPU virtual server featuring 2 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of local SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance for budget-conscious workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx22 is a shared-CPU virtual server with 2 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 80 GB SSD storage, optimized for entry-level workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared-CPU instance featuring strong single-core performance and local SSD storage for lightweight workloads and development environments.
bullet_points: 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
4.0 GB of system memory and 80 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-core performance ranking in the top tier
Weak multi-core scaling and database performance due to shared resources
Best suited for development, testing, and lightweight background tasks
